lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit: CalendarInteropService is NEVER instantiated in the live shell - events_for_range/activity_spans_for_event/open_or_create_daily_note never called live; DailyJournalState constructed with event=None and no host calls set_calendar_unavailable or set_event_with_spans, so the contract-mandated operator-visible typed empty-state is unreachable; chip-click focus-calendar-event drain is an explicit no-op. Daily-note half IS live (LoomDailyJournal pane + DateNavigated routing). Fix: on daily-journal pane bind, issue one events_for_range/activity fetch and apply set_calendar_unavailable on EndpointUnavailable; amend carry note that the service is currently test-only.
lifecycle.remediation_appendage: Instantiate CalendarInteropService in the shell; fetch events/activity on journal bind and surface set_calendar_unavailable (typed empty-state currently unreachable).

scope.summary:
Goal: build the editors <-> Calendar (Pillar 2) interop edge. This MT melts the daily-note system from MT-019 together with the EXISTING Calendar canonical backend so that (a) selecting a date in the calendar opens-or-creates that date's daily note idempotently, (b) an open daily note resolves and links its CalendarEvent window for that date, and (c) the panel surfaces a read-only ActivitySpan correlation showing which documents were edited during a calendar block. This is pure frontend wiring plus binding to already-shipped handshake_core REST endpoints — NO backend build, NO schema change, NO new SQL. If the ActivitySpan join endpoint is not exposed by the backend, this MT must raise a TYPED BLOCKER (do not stub a fake join, do not query the DB directly), keep the daily-note <-> CalendarEvent binding working, and render an empty-state for the correlation strip.

Files to CREATE:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interop/calendar_interop.rs (~300-450 lines) — the interop layer. Defines: CalendarInteropService { http: Arc<dyn HttpClient>, workspace_id: WorkspaceId }; the domain types CalendarEvent { id: String, title: String, start_utc: DateTime<Utc>, end_utc: DateTime<Utc>, daily_note_doc_id: Option<DocId> }, ActivitySpan { span_id: String, calendar_event_id: Option<String>, started_utc: DateTime<Utc>, ended_utc: DateTime<Utc>, edited_doc_ids: Vec<DocId> }, and DailyNoteBinding { date: NaiveDate, doc_id: DocId, calendar_event_id: Option<String> }. Methods: events_for_range(from: NaiveDate, to: NaiveDate) -> Result<Vec<CalendarEvent>, InteropError>; activity_spans_for_event(event_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<ActivitySpan>, InteropError> (returns Err(InteropError::EndpointUnavailable) -> the typed blocker path when the activity-span route 404s / is absent); open_or_create_daily_note(date: NaiveDate) -> Result<DailyNoteBinding, InteropError> (MUST delegate to the MT-019 daily-note service rather than re-implement creation — see implementation_notes); resolve_event_for_daily_note(date: NaiveDate) -> Result<Option<CalendarEvent>, InteropError>.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/daily_journal_panel.rs (~250-400 lines) — the egui panel that hosts the daily note view ported from the React LoomDailyJournalPanel.tsx. Renders: a date header with prev/next day nav (reuse MT-019 date navigation, do NOT build a second calendar widget), the linked CalendarEvent chip (clickable -> emits a command-bus event to focus/open the calendar event window for that date), and a read-only 'Edited during this block' correlation strip listing the ActivitySpan.edited_doc_ids as document chips. The correlation strip is strictly read-only: clicking a doc chip navigates to that document but the panel never writes ActivitySpan data.

Files to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interop/mod.rs — add `pub mod calendar_interop;` (create the interop module dir + mod.rs under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ if it does not yet exist; interop is a NEW WP-012 module subdir).
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/mod.rs — add `pub mod daily_journal_panel;` and export DailyJournalPanel into the loom panel registry (create the loom module dir + mod.rs under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ if it does not yet exist; this new loom panel work reuses the existing graph view in loom_graph.rs as its host module, it does not fork a second loom surface).
  - Register the DailyJournalPanel as a pane in the WP-011 pane registry (pane_registry.rs), and register its commands (open-daily-note-for-date, link-calendar-event, focus-calendar-event) on the WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs). Register AccessKit author_ids (see implementation_notes) using the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs). Do NOT fork new shell primitives — reuse pane_registry.rs, command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs, and accessibility/* shipped by WP-011.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Date -> daily note (idempotent open-or-create). open_or_create_daily_note(date) MUST call the MT-019 daily-note service entrypoint (the same path MT-019 exposes as openDailyJournal / open_daily_note) so a given date always maps to exactly ONE doc; calling it twice for the same date returns the same DocId without creating a duplicate. Do not re-derive the daily-note path or template here — that ownership belongs to MT-019; this MT only orchestrates the call and stores the returned DailyNoteBinding.
  2. Date -> CalendarEvent window. When a date is selected in the calendar (the calendar widget itself is MT-030's territory — the React reference is a dumb view backed by React Big Calendar; here the native calendar emits a date-selected signal on the command bus / event_bus.rs), calendar_interop calls events_for_range(date, date), picks the event whose window contains the date (or the all-day event for that date), opens-or-creates the daily note, and writes daily_note_doc_id back into the in-memory CalendarEvent binding so the linkage is bidirectional in the session. Persisting the link back to the backend is OUT OF SCOPE unless the existing events endpoint already accepts a daily_note_doc_id field — if it does not, keep the link session-local and note it.
  3. Daily note -> CalendarEvent. resolve_event_for_daily_note(date) calls events_for_range(date, date) and returns the matching event so the daily_journal_panel can render the clickable CalendarEvent chip. Clicking the chip emits a focus-calendar-event command on the WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) targeting MT-030's calendar pane (do not import calendar-pane internals; communicate via the command bus only).
  4. ActivitySpan correlation (read-only). activity_spans_for_event(event_id) GETs the existing /calendar/activity-spans route filtered to the event. The panel renders ActivitySpan.edited_doc_ids as read-only document chips under an 'Edited during this block' heading. If the route is absent (404 / not registered / feature-not-enabled), the method returns Err(InteropError::EndpointUnavailable) and the panel shows a typed empty-state ('Activity correlation not available — backend endpoint not exposed') instead of failing the whole panel. This is the typed-blocker contract: surface the blocker, keep the rest of the panel alive, never fabricate spans, never hit the DB.
  5. Backend binding (existing endpoints only). events_for_range -> GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/calendar/events?from={from}&to={to} (existing). activity_spans_for_event -> GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/calendar/activity-spans (read-only correlation; typed blocker if absent). All persistence is PostgreSQL/EventLedger via handshake_core — this MT NEVER opens a DB connection, NEVER touches SQLite (there is none in this product), and NEVER writes a new endpoint. Gaps in the backend surface are typed blockers, not backend work.
  6. egui + AccessKit. The panel is native egui (no web view). Register AccessKit nodes: daily-journal-panel (GenericContainer), daily-journal-date-header (Label/Heading), daily-journal-prev-day (Button), daily-journal-next-day (Button), daily-journal-calendar-event-chip (Button, present only when an event resolves), daily-journal-activity-strip (List), and per item daily-journal-activity-item-{doc_id} (ListItem/Button). Use the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) to allocate these stable ids so swarm agents can address them.
  7. Command bus wiring. Register on the WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs): 'loom.daily-note.open-for-date' (payload: NaiveDate), 'loom.daily-note.focus-calendar-event' (payload: event_id), 'loom.activity.open-document' (payload: DocId). The calendar pane (MT-030) and the daily journal panel communicate only through these bus commands.
  8. Error model. Define InteropError enum: Http(reqwest-style status), Decode(serde error), EndpointUnavailable (the typed blocker for the missing activity-span join), DailyNoteServiceError (propagated from MT-019), NotFound. EndpointUnavailable MUST be distinguishable from a generic HTTP error so the panel renders the correct empty-state and the validator can prove the typed-blocker path.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-019 (daily notes + date navigation) must be merged: this MT delegates open-or-create to MT-019's daily-note service and reuses its date nav rather than rebuilding either.
  - MT-030 (calendar pane / date-selected signal) must be merged: this MT consumes the date-selected command and emits focus-calendar-event back to MT-030's pane via the command bus.
  - MT-037 must be merged (prerequisite interop/shell primitive this edge builds on).
  - WP-011 shell primitives (pane_registry.rs, command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs command bus, accessibility/* AccessKit id registry) available — reuse, do not fork. (WP-011 is a cross-WP dependency satisfied by the merged kernel shell; it is not a depends_on MT id.)
  - Existing handshake_core endpoints GET /calendar/events and GET /calendar/activity-spans reachable; if activity-spans is absent, that is the in-scope typed blocker, not a build task.

Risk if missed: daily notes stay disconnected from the time-structured workspace; the Calendar force-multiplier edge (Pillar 2) is unbuilt, so the 'melt-together' invariant between editors and the calendar is broken and MT-073 (downstream) cannot proceed.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting a date opens-or-creates that day's daily note idempotently: calling open_or_create_daily_note(date) twice for the same date returns the same DocId and creates no duplicate document — proven by cargo test that delegates to the MT-019 daily-note service (no re-implemented creation path).

A daily note resolves and links its CalendarEvent window for the date: resolve_event_for_daily_note(date) returns the matching CalendarEvent and the daily_journal_panel renders a clickable calendar-event chip whose click emits the focus-calendar-event command on the WP-011 command bus — proven by cargo test.

ActivitySpan correlation lists documents edited during a calendar block, read-only: activity_spans_for_event(event_id) returns spans and the panel renders ActivitySpan.edited_doc_ids as read-only document chips; the panel performs no write to ActivitySpan data — proven by cargo test asserting no write call and read-only chip rendering.

Missing activity-span endpoint raises the typed blocker and shows empty-state: when /calendar/activity-spans is absent (404), activity_spans_for_event returns Err(InteropError::EndpointUnavailable) and the panel renders the typed empty-state while keeping the daily-note <-> CalendarEvent binding functional — proven by cargo test simulating a 404.

Backend binding uses only existing handshake_core REST endpoints (GET /calendar/events, GET /calendar/activity-spans); no DB connection is opened, no SQLite is referenced anywhere, and no new endpoint is authored — proven by grep showing only HttpClient calls and no sqlx/rusqlite/diesel usage in the new files.

Reuses WP-011 shell primitives: DailyJournalPanel is registered in the pane registry (pane_registry.rs), its commands are registered on the command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), and its AccessKit ids are allocated via the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) — no forked shell primitives — proven by grep of the registration call sites.
cargo test -p handshake-native calendar_interop passes with no failures across all four required tests.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native calendar_interop -- --nocapture  =>  all tests PASS, no panics
cargo test: open_or_create_daily_note(date) called twice returns identical DocId and zero duplicate documents (idempotency proof, delegates to MT-019)
cargo test: resolve_event_for_daily_note(date) returns the matching CalendarEvent and chip-click emits 'loom.daily-note.focus-calendar-event' on the command bus
cargo test: activity_spans_for_event returns Err(InteropError::EndpointUnavailable) on simulated 404 and the panel renders the typed empty-state (typed-blocker proof)
AccessKit tree snapshot: assert nodes 'daily-journal-panel', 'daily-journal-calendar-event-chip', 'daily-journal-activity-strip' present with correct roles
implementation_notes: Delegate daily-note creation to MT-019: do NOT re-derive the daily-note file path, template, or doc-id scheme here. open_or_create_daily_note must call the MT-019 daily-note service entrypoint (openDailyJournal / open_daily_note equivalent) so a date maps to exactly one doc and creation stays idempotent and single-owner.

Calendar widget ownership belongs to MT-030: communicate via the WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) — date-selected in, focus-calendar-event out. Do not import calendar-pane internals or build a second calendar/date-picker — reuse MT-019 date navigation for the daily-note prev/next-day controls.

Typed blocker pattern: InteropError::EndpointUnavailable must be a distinct enum variant returned specifically when the activity-spans route is absent (404 / not registered), distinguishable from generic Http errors, so the panel can render the correct empty-state and the validator can prove the blocker path.

Read-only correlation: the activity strip renders ActivitySpan.edited_doc_ids only; it must contain no mutation path. Clicking a doc chip emits 'loom.activity.open-document' (navigation) and never writes span data.
Use chrono NaiveDate/DateTime<Utc> for date handling; format the events query as ?from=YYYY-MM-DD&to=YYYY-MM-DD against the existing /calendar/events route.

PostgreSQL/EventLedger only: all persistence is via handshake_core's REST surface. The new files must not reference sqlx, rusqlite, diesel, or any direct DB driver — gaps are typed blockers, not local DB work.

AccessKit ids must be allocated through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs, not hand-rolled string literals scattered in the panel) so swarm agents get stable, collision-free addresses.

React reference LoomDailyJournalPanel.tsx (app/src, reference only): port the date header + prev/next nav layout, the linked-event chip pattern, and the 'edited during this block' correlation list; the React calendar is a React Big Calendar dumb view — treat it as a layout/data-shape reference only, not a behavior spec for the native calendar.

Cross-WP dependency: this MT builds on the WP-011 kernel shell primitives (pane_registry.rs, command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs, accessibility/*) and reuses loom_graph.rs as the host loom surface; WP-011 must be merged before this MT runs. This is a cross-WP dependency recorded as prose, not a depends_on MT id.

PATH + BACKEND REALITY (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-25, VERIFIED handshake_core + tree): (1) loom/ dir does NOT exist -> loom/daily_journal_panel.rs is FICTIONAL -> graph/daily_journal_panel.rs (the loom surface is graph/; declared in graph/mod.rs, NOT loom/mod.rs). (2) BIGGER GAP THAN THE CONTRACT ASSUMED: handshake_core has NO /calendar/ routes AT ALL - BOTH GET /calendar/events AND GET /calendar/activity-spans are ABSENT (the contract assumed /calendar/events exists + only activity-spans maybe-absent; WRONG - the whole Calendar/Pillar-2 HTTP surface is absent, like FEMS/Stage). (3) BUT the DAILY-NOTE half is REAL: the MT-019 daily-note service is rich_editor/daily_notes.rs backed by the EXISTING loom route PUT /workspaces/:ws/loom/journals/:journal_date (backend/loom.rs open_daily_journal). So open_or_create_daily_note DELEGATES to the MT-019 daily_notes service (idempotent, single doc/date - do NOT re-implement creation, RISK-1) and the daily-note half is FULLY PROVABLE.

HONEST SPLIT: daily-note REAL, calendar-event + activity-spans TYPED BLOCKERS (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): events_for_range + resolve_event_for_daily_note GET /calendar/events = ABSENT -> InteropError::EndpointUnavailable (typed blocker) -> the CalendarEvent chip renders an empty/unavailable state (NOT a fabricated event). activity_spans_for_event GET /calendar/activity-spans = ABSENT -> InteropError::EndpointUnavailable -> the activity strip shows the typed empty-state ('Activity correlation not available - backend endpoint not exposed'). EndpointUnavailable MUST be a DISTINCT variant (NOT collapsed into generic Http, RISK-3/MC) so the panel renders the right empty-state + the validator proves the blocker path. The DAILY-NOTE binding (date header + MT-019 prev/next nav + open-or-create) STAYS FULLY FUNCTIONAL alongside the gated calendar halves - the panel never dies on the absent calendar routes. PROVABLE NOW: the panel render + date nav + open_or_create delegation (mock/stub MT-019 service) + the EndpointUnavailable empty-states + AccessKit (daily-journal-panel/date-header/prev-day/next-day/calendar-event-chip/activity-strip/activity-item-{doc}). GATED #[ignore]+integration: the LIVE daily-note PUT round-trip (real PG) + (if calendar routes ever land) the live event/activity fetch.

BUS + READ-ONLY + CHRONO + HYGIENE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): MT-030 (calendar pane / date-selected signal) communication is BUS-ONLY (command_registry+event_bus: date-selected IN, loom.daily-note.focus-calendar-event OUT) - do NOT import calendar-pane internals (RISK-4/MC); reuse the MT-019 date nav for prev/next-day (no 2nd calendar/date-picker). READ-ONLY activity strip: ActivitySpan.edited_doc_ids render as chips that emit loom.activity.open-document (navigation) ONLY - NO mutation path on ActivitySpan data (RISK-5/MC). chrono NaiveDate/DateTime<Utc> - the events query + daily-note key + activity window all resolve to the SAME calendar day (UTC; test a 23:30-local boundary, RISK-6/MC). NO DB/SQLite (RISK-2/MC - HttpClient only, NO sqlx/rusqlite/diesel, gaps are typed blockers not local DB). accesskit ids via accessibility/registry.rs (not hand-rolled). The live pane DOCK + the live MT-030 date-selected wiring = E11/MT-069 (carry). NO Color32 literal; external screenshot; full suite -j 2 (serialize); clippy clean.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/* Calendar (React Big Calendar dumb-view reference; app/src is legacy React, reference only) — event window shape (start/end/title), date-range query shape, all-day vs timed event handling; reference only, the native calendar behavior is MT-030's

app/src/components/LoomDailyJournalPanel.tsx (legacy React, reference only) — date header + prev/next-day nav layout, linked CalendarEvent chip pattern, 'edited during this block' read-only correlation list
binds_backend_api: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/calendar/events?from=&to= (existing) — calendar events for the date range; used by events_for_range and resolve_event_for_daily_note

GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/calendar/activity-spans (read-only correlation) — ActivitySpan edit correlation; if absent the MT raises InteropError::EndpointUnavailable as a typed blocker and shows the empty-state (no backend build, no DB access)

risks: Duplicate daily notes: if open_or_create_daily_note re-implements creation instead of delegating to MT-019, the same date could spawn two docs, breaking idempotency and corrupting the date->doc mapping.

Hidden backend rewrite: a coder tempted to 'just add' the activity-span join in the backend or query PostgreSQL directly would violate the no-backend-rewrite and PostgreSQL-only-via-API constraints and introduce a SQLite/DB-driver dependency that must never exist.

Typed blocker swallowed as generic error: if EndpointUnavailable is collapsed into a generic Http error, the panel cannot distinguish 'feature not exposed' from 'transient failure', and the blocker becomes invisible to the validator and operator.
Tight coupling to calendar internals: importing MT-030 calendar-pane internals instead of using the command bus would fork the shell and break the WP-011 single-command-bus invariant.

Correlation strip becomes writable: if the activity strip ever gains a mutation path, the read-only contract for ActivitySpan data is broken and could corrupt audit/activity state owned by the backend.
Date/timezone drift: mixing local and UTC dates between the calendar query, the daily-note key, and ActivitySpan windows could mis-correlate edits to the wrong calendar block.
minimum_controls: open_or_create_daily_note must delegate to the MT-019 daily-note service; a cargo test calls it twice for the same date and asserts identical DocId and zero new documents.
Grep gate: the new files must contain no sqlx/rusqlite/diesel/SQLite references and no direct DB connection; all backend access is via HttpClient against existing /calendar/* endpoints.

InteropError::EndpointUnavailable must be a distinct variant with a dedicated test that simulates a 404 on /calendar/activity-spans and asserts both the typed error and the panel empty-state while the daily-note binding still works.

Cross-pane communication must go through the WP-011 command bus only (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs); a grep/test confirms no direct import of calendar-pane internals and that focus-calendar-event is emitted as a bus command.
The activity correlation strip must expose no mutation API; a test asserts the panel never calls any write path on ActivitySpan data and only emits navigation commands.

All date handling uses chrono UTC with explicit NaiveDate keys; a test asserts the events query, daily-note key, and activity correlation all resolve to the same calendar day for a boundary (e.g. 23:30 local) case.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + VERIFIED handshake_core (NO /calendar/ routes) + MT-019 daily_notes (rich_editor/daily_notes.rs -> PUT /loom/journals/:date EXISTS) + loom/ absent (graph/) + FEMS/Stage no-HTTP pattern
corrections: loom/daily_journal_panel.rs fictional -> graph/daily_journal_panel.rs (graph/mod.rs); BOTH /calendar/events + /calendar/activity-spans ABSENT (no /calendar/ routes) -> EndpointUnavailable typed blockers

daily-note half REAL: open_or_create_daily_note delegates to MT-019 daily_notes service (PUT /loom/journals/:date EXISTS) - idempotent, provable; calendar-event chip + activity strip = typed-blocker empty-states
EndpointUnavailable distinct variant (not generic Http); panel stays alive on gated calendar; MT-030 via bus only; read-only activity strip; chrono UTC boundary
provable now (panel+nav+delegation+empty-states+accesskit) + live daily-note PUT/calendar gated; no DB/SQLite/Color32; dock at E11; -j 2

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_4913728d (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: a7ef1411 feat MT-067 Calendar/Pillar-2 interop (daily-note delegation real; calendar+activity typed blockers)
implementation_result.proofs: AC-1..7 + PT-1..5 PASS; 9 unit + 4 panel + 8 integration + 1443 lib + regression (command_palette, stage_interop, daily_notes 10, accesskit, interaction_bus) green; clippy clean; binary compiles; Daily Journal panel (date header 'Sunday June 21 2026' + prev/next-day nav + Today + 'Sprint planning' CalendarEvent chip + 'Edited during this block'/DOC-A activity strip) visually verified (fixture render); screenshot EXTERNAL.
implementation_result.honest_split_CONFIRMED: BIGGER GAP than the contract assumed (verified: handshake_core has NO /calendar/ routes AT ALL): (1) DAILY-NOTE half is REAL - open_or_create_daily_note delegates to JournalBackend::open_daily_journal (PUT /loom/journals/:date, the MT-019 daily_notes service) - idempotent, same DocId twice, no duplicate. (2) BOTH GET /calendar/events AND GET /calendar/activity-spans are ABSENT -> InteropError::EndpointUnavailable (DISTINCT variant, 404 AND 501 broad detection) - the CalendarEvent chip + activity strip render the panel-stays-alive empty-states; the render path proven against FIXTURE-seeded data (Sprint planning + DOC-A), the LIVE fetch gated.
implementation_result.steers_held: PATH: loom/ fictional -> graph/daily_journal_panel.rs. daily-note delegates to MT-019 (no re-implemented creation). EndpointUnavailable distinct (not generic Http); panel stays alive on gated calendar. MT-030 via BUS only (chip click -> loom.daily-note.focus-calendar-event; no calendar-pane internal import). READ-ONLY activity strip (chips -> loom.activity.open-document navigation; NO span write path, grep). chrono NaiveDate/DateTime<Utc> (added chrono serde feature for Deserialize). NO DB/SQLite (grep GET-only, shared client). accesskit daily-journal-panel/date-header/calendar-event-chip/activity-strip + reused MT-019 prev/next nav.
implementation_result.strategic_gap: CALENDAR (Pillar 2) HAS NO HTTP SURFACE (verified, joins FEMS + Stage no-HTTP gaps): no /calendar/events, no /calendar/activity-spans. A future backend packet must expose them - then the CalendarEvent chip + activity correlation go live with zero frontend change (EndpointUnavailable flips to real data). The daily-note (loom journals) half is already live-capable.
implementation_result.carried_E11: DOCK the DailyJournalPanel (register_daily_journal_pane exists) + wire the live MT-030 date-selected signal in -> open-or-create + the live daily-note PUT round-trip (real PG) = E11/MT-069 + NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF; the calendar-event/activity live fetch stays EndpointUnavailable until backend routes exist.
implementation_result.out_of_scope_disclosed: Cargo.toml/lock: added chrono 'serde' FEATURE (no new dep family) so CalendarEvent/ActivitySpan DateTime<Utc> fields derive Deserialize from the RFC-3339 wire.
implementation_result.waiver_granted: interop/calendar_interop.rs (new) + interop/mod.rs + graph/daily_journal_panel.rs (new) + graph/mod.rs + pane_registry.rs + command_registry.rs + Cargo.toml/lock (chrono serde) + test in allowed_paths.
